claim.label: Replacement Pressure
claim.query: volunteer gaelic patriotic movement duty nation labour protestant unionist minority
claim.counter_query: protestant presbyterian minority labour civic municipal foreign travel commerce
claim.thesis: Track whether plurality and civic language are displaced by a more disciplined nationalist editorial vocabulary.

to control, or in the least degree influence, the civil an

religious rights of the Protestant minority? Surely it

is not anticipated that they will emulate the doings of

the Irish Episcopalians during the seventeenth century

as recorded in our last article—that they will prohibit

the public exercise of the Protestant religion—banish

Protestant clergymen and schoolmasters—and compe

Protestants to attend Catholic churches?

Assuming Irish Catholics to have the disposition to

thus act, would they have the power?

Are the 25,000 soldiers stationed in Ireland, and who

are mostly Protestants, to be replaced by 25,000 others,

mostly Catholics? Are the dreadnoughts, cruisers, and

destroyers to be divided between Ireland and Great

Britain in proportion to population, and placed at the

lian societies have taken a vastly broader and loftier

We attempted to make the Christmas Number of

the LEADER rather ‘s Christmas Annual this year. It

Was current and up to date-—sa much so that the Post

Office did not dare to'challenge its right to pass on a

ha’penny inland postage—but it is turrent for the

season rather' than for the week. The sale of it still

supporting_documents.chunk_text:
the time the responsibility for trouble might be very

much in question as between employers and employees.

A Labour movement of that kind is unthinkable.

The first prominent appearance in Ireland of the

organised sympathetic strike was about two years ago

when a timber dispute precipitated the great railway

strike. ~ We expressed ourselves pretty freely then

against an organised sympathetic strike, which is in-

dustrially absurd and impossible; though we also con-

demned the Great Southern Railway for not giving con-

sidering time for those concerned, including the public,

before things were precipitated. Many of the men, in

the Great Southern Railway were smarting under the

since re-exported Hastings Dent. The Great Southern

are now rid of Dent. About two years ago we more

the poor newspaper camera brigade off their balance.
We doubt if the best friends of some of the titled ladies
recently snapped felt that any debt-of gratitude was
due fo the snapper.
